content.text: Commits & PRs
is the part of Claude Code workflows that helps turn finished code changes into a proper git commit and then package them into a pull request for review.
What it means
A
commit
is the saved snapshot of your code changes in git. A
pull request
is the reviewable proposal to merge those changes into another branch, usually after pushing the branch to a remote repository.

Claude Code can help with both steps by:
- Reviewing the current diff.
- Writing a commit message that matches the repo’s style.
- Staging the right files.
- Creating a branch if needed.
- Pushing the branch.
- Opening a pull request with a useful summary.
Why it matters

This workflow reduces repetitive git work and keeps development moving without constant context switching. It is especially useful when you want Claude to help with the mechanical parts of shipping code, while you still stay in control of the final result.
Typical flow
Step
What happens
1. Review changes
Claude inspects staged and unstaged diffs.
2. Create commit
Claude forms a commit with an appropriate message.
3. Push branch
Claude sends the branch to the remote repo.
4. Open PR
Claude creates a pull request, often using GitHub CLI.
Common commands

Claude Code supports workflows where you can ask it to create a PR directly, or guide it step by step through commit and PR creation. There are also helper commands in some setups for commit, push, and PR creation as a single workflow.
Best practices
Make the code complete first
, then commit and open the PR.
Review the diff before approval
, even if Claude wrote the message.
Use descriptive branch names and commit messages
so the history stays easy to follow.
Add a PR summary and test notes
so reviewers know what changed and how to verify it.
Trade-offs

Claude can save time on git operations, but it should not replace human review. You still need to confirm that the commit groups the right changes and that the PR description accurately reflects the work.
Mental model
Think of Claude Code here as a
release assistant
: it takes the edited files, packages them cleanly, and prepares them for review, while you remain the final decision-maker.
Key takeaways
Commits save your changes; PRs present them for review.
Claude Code can help create both from the same working branch.
The workflow reduces manual git steps and context switching.
Human review still matters for message quality and correctness.
